claim: The YOLO demand router schema readiness correctly blocks PRD readiness when evidence agents report missing evidence, clarify recommendations, block recommendations, or failed statuses, as verified by existing tests in router.ts.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function creates blockers for missing, clarify, block, and failed statuses
evidence.why: This is the core logic that converts evidence agent issues into PRD blockers. Line 496 creates EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING blocker when missing.length > 0. Line 503 creates EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED or EVIDENCE_AGENT_BLOCKED for clarify/block recommendations.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues for each evidence agent result and adds all issues to blockers
evidence.why: This integration ensures that every blocker from evidenceResultBlockingIssues is added to the blockerList, which determines prd_ready.

evidence.summary: prd_ready is set to blockers.length === 0, meaning any blocker prevents PRD readiness
evidence.why: This is the final gate that ensures prd_ready is false when any evidence agent has reported missing, clarify, block, or failed status.

evidence.summary: Test verifies that missing evidence and clarify recommendations block PRD readiness
evidence.why: This test confirms the current implementation blocks PRD readiness when cross-checker returns missing array and recommendation: 'clarify'. Lines 350-352 assert prd_ready === false and check for EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED blockers.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch calls inspectDemandPrdReadiness with agent results and forces prd_ready false if boundary violations occur
evidence.why: This shows how evidence-dispatch uses inspectDemandPrdReadiness and enforces prd_ready === false when blockers exist.

assumptions: The demand description may be confirming existing behavior rather than reporting a bug, as the current implementation already blocks PRD readiness for these cases
The demand may be motivated by wanting explicit verification or documentation that this blocking behavior is working correctly
risks: If this claim is wrong, PRDs could be generated from incomplete evidence when they should be blocked
Missing evidence could be treated as optional and not block PRD generation as intended

result.notes: Current implementation in router.ts correctly blocks PRD readiness when evidence agents report missing, clarify, block, or failed statuses. The evidenceResultBlockingIssues function (lines 481-510) creates appropriate blockers, inspectDemandPrdReadiness (lines 717-732) adds them to the blocker list, and prd_ready (line 756) is set to false when blockers.length > 0. Test at lines 313-353 verifies this behavior.
